mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending power and equity risk appetite, which lifts S&P 500 prices within the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which compresses credit spreads and validates corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 50 basis points over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or INDPRO and HOUST diverge for more than two consecutive months without SPX reversal.

mechanism_narrative: During early-stage monetary normalization, rising short-term rates reflect improving economic fundamentals rather than inflation fears, supporting equity multiples and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns turn negative when FEDFUNDS rises alongside CPIAUCSL exceeding a 2.5 percent annualized pace, indicating inflation-driven rate hikes rather than growth-driven normalization.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in retail and food services sales exceeding 1.5 percent triggers a repricing of near-term earnings growth expectations, immediately lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when RSAFS crosses the 1.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll growth consistently signals labor market resilience, which reduces recession probabilities and supports equity risk premiums, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as PAYEMS trends higher.
failure_condition: Walk-forward predictive R-squared remains below 0.01 over a 5-year period, or SPX 5-day returns show no significant difference between months with payroll growth above and below 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll additions and sub-5 percent unemployment create a wage-price spiral risk that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately capping equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to predict a volatility regime shift (VIX > 20) within the 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces real GDP growth by more than 0.5 percentage points, supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints emerge, compressing corporate margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO and GDPC1 growth rates shows no correlation with subsequent SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or margin compression indicators fail to rise during the signal period.
